title: magnolia liliifera
intro: Magnolia liliifera, also known as the 'Queen of the Night', is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, characterized by its large, showy white flowers that bloom only at night. The bark and leaves of the Magnolia liliifera plant are used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices, for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are also used in traditional remedies such as ointments and salves.
